threat_actor_profile.motivation: Espionage and disruption of transportation infrastructure

overview.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Authentication issues
overview.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Web, Phishing, Brute-force attack, Vulnerability exploit, Malware, Man in The Middle(MiTM)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2024-47575 [CWE-22]: Unspecified vulnerability in email client allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted message.
stage.description: The Outer Space campaign targets employees within the Transportation Systems industry with spear-phishing emails containing malicious attachments. The attachment exploits a vulnerability in the recipient's email client (CVE-2024-47575), allowing the attacker to execute arbitrary code and gain initial access to the target network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2024-53104 [CWE-94]: Improper control of generation of code ('code injection') in PowerShell allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via unspecified vectors.
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 579 - Replace Winlogon Helper DLL
stage.description: Upon successful execution of the initial payload, the attacker uses PowerShell to download and execute the next stage of the attack. This is achieved by exploiting a code injection vulnerability in PowerShell (CVE-2024-53104), allowing the attacker to run arbitrary commands and download additional malware onto the compromised system.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker creates a scheduled task to maintain persistence on the compromised system. This task runs periodically, checking for signs of the attacker's presence and re-establishing access if necessary. To bypass authentication controls, the attacker employs brute-force techniques against weak or stolen credentials (CWE-287).

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker escalates privileges by exploiting weaknesses in the target's User Account Control (UAC) implementation. By gathering information about domain accounts and their permissions (CAPEC-575), the attacker identifies privileged accounts and exploits vulnerabilities in UAC to elevate their own privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: To evade detection, the attacker signs malicious code using legitimate certificates obtained through unauthorized means. This technique makes the malware appear trustworthy, allowing it to bypass security measures designed to detect and prevent the execution of unsigned or untrusted code.

stage.name: Impact
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.description: The final stage of the attack involves disrupting the target organization's operations by manipulating hardware or software components. The attacker exploits a weakness in the system maintenance process, causing changes to be made to technology, products, or components for the purpose of achieving the desired impact on the target organization's operations.

overview.summary: The Outer Space campaign targets the Transportation Systems industry with a multi-stage attack, starting with spear-phishing emails and culminating in the disruption of the target organization's operations. Throughout the attack, the campaign employs various techniques to maintain persistence, escalate privileges, and evade detection.
